posts.name: Alan Eden
posts.cooked: Anyone tried any barrel samples yet, any gossip on style or quality. Futures out soon so curious

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: Wait until the weekend of March 5/6, you’ll get plenty of opinions after the assemblage tasting. You don’t have to pull the trigger on future typically until after the second tasting in May.

posts.name: TR
posts.cooked:
Ridge has charged my card for futures orders in late March the past few years. I will be there on Saturday the 5th, looking forward to getting an early look at the 15s.

I’m also hoping they do the usual 20% off a case for event weekends, I need to stock up on the 13 estate cab and 13 Lytton.

posts.name: Ashish
posts.cooked:
Alan Rath:

Wait until the weekend of March 5/6, you’ll get plenty of opinions after the assemblage tasting. You don’t have to pull the trigger on future typically until after the second tasting in May.

March is the component tasting, right? I didn’t think the assemblage tasting with blends was until later, like April?

posts.name: Sc0tt F!tzger@ld
posts.cooked: This has got to be one of the safest future purchases in all wine.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
AAgrawal:

March is the component tasting, right? I didn’t think the assemblage tasting with blends was until later, like April?

Yes, but they’ll have a “first assemblage” to try, which is typically about 90% of the final wine, I think.

posts.name: Chris Seiber
posts.cooked:
It’s the vintage of the year for Monte Bello. Back up the truck.

I keed. It is indeed a very safe bet year to year.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: I’m looking forward to trying it. 2015 was one of the warmer years we’ve had in quite a while (though not stinking hot, by any means). I expect the wines from up on the ridge to be slightly larger framed this year.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: When I corresponded with Eric Baugher at the end of last year he was very happy with the vintage and made comparisons with the 1995 - which is drinking very well right now.

posts.name: A. So
posts.cooked: I just don’t understand how this could be a bad wine. I’ve never had a bad Monte Bello. Especially at futures pricing, easy no-brainer of a purchase.

posts.name: Tim F@itsch
posts.cooked:
Just got the email with confirmation of my allocation. I can’t remember what 2014 was but $110 seems like more than I remember paying in the past. It’s still an easy yes at that price. I’m a fairly active auction buyer of older MB vintages and have noticed prices creeping up quite a bit lately. $150 for 2009 on Winebid right now. I think I was paying less than that for 1991s just five years ago.

Of note: “Due to limited production, allocation increases will not be accepted with this vintage.”

posts.cooked:

posts.name: Scott Jameson
posts.cooked: Last year was $105/750 and $213/mag …

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: It has been going up about $5/year for the past several years. Obviously they are trying to extract more of the released market value without putting too much of a hit on futures purchasers, but the price pressure is definitely there now.

posts.name: Glenn L e v i n e
posts.cooked: $57/$110 this time around for the 2015 and all you can get is what you bought in 2014.
posts.display_username: Glenn L e v i n e

posts.name: Ethan Abraham
posts.cooked: For those curious, for 13 and 14 you could only increase to 1 case (i guess assuming you were less than that), 12 no increase allowed, 11 and 10 was up to 2 cases.

posts.name: John Davis
posts.cooked:
Personally, a no brainer for me. Even with the relatively minimal increases.

JD

posts.name: Alan Eden
posts.cooked:
Question

Ive bought the last two vintages but obviously not tried them yet, how different is MB to say a Moulds vineyard , Beckstroffer or other bigger style Napa.

posts.name: A. So
posts.cooked:
Alan Eden:

Question

Ive bought the last two vintages but obviously not tried them yet, how different is MB to say a Moulds vineyard , Beckstroffer or other bigger style Napa.

Big difference. It’s actually good.

posts.name: Mark Golodetz
posts.cooked:
Tasted a barrel sample last week as well as the 2014. I intend to buy both.

The 2014 is more opulent, rich and perfectly ripe. Acidity was slightly less than the more sleek 2015, but both showed great complexity, and lovely red fruit. Finishes in both were long.

participants.name: Glenn L e v i n e
